Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Continued To Jul-03-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #6, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Machado for and with Edward Watson.

The Court stated that we are here on the 6th accounting requesting fees and preliminary distribution, that there are interesting issues, that there is no allocation to the sub-trusts, that it is strange that Ms. Dolch was hired to handle non-trust issues, that she was paid without court approval and that some of it was reverted back to the law firm and that issue of guideline fees vs. hourly fees. Mr. Edwards provided information on the family background, that he initially had power of attorney, that in December of 2009 he became the successor trustee, that they had tried to get the son to step forward but he would not respond, that it became necessary for a conservatorship to be established for Mr. Gicovate with Ms. Dolch as conservator, that there were many problems including lack of assets, that they sold one condominium but Mrs. Gicovate would not let the others be sold, that they eventually were sold, that there were tax issues on the sale, that they reinvested the proceeds, that they should have funded the sub-trusts, that everything goes to the son anyway, that normally the trust would not be under court supervision and that he had to use his power of attorney to obtain Mr. Gicovate's teacher pension as they would not accept his position as trustee. Mr. Machado stated that in regards to the sub-trusts, there was 1 year and two months between the death of Mr. & Mrs. Gicovate, that regarding the concern of Mr. Dolch and her varying roles the examiner's notes (2a payments to firm) Mr. Edwards had hired the firm and that the root of the matter is that these were non trust issues that needed addressing. Mr. Edwards stated that TIA Cref would not deal with him as a trustee, only as a power of attorney. Mr. Machado stated that the fee's dealt with obtaining authority here, that there were 2 payments for tax fees, one which was around the time of death of Mrs. Gicovate and the other for 2014 and that ultimately it all goes to the same beneficiary. Mr. Edwards stated that he has met with Mr. Sturgis and that he had no objections but wanted to know when he would get his inheritance. Mr. Machado addressed the trustee fees stating that they had been cut back in the past and that they are asking to remain under a % based fee for the duration. The Court took the accounting petition under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-30-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gary Machado for and with (petitioner/trustee) Edward Watson.

The Court stated that it needs information on the $38,000.00 and $27,441.00 distributed to Ms. Dolch and that it needs an accounting. Mr. Machado stated that they have a final accounting coming up for 2015, that they could include the information in that accounting, that it will be filed within 2 months and that the trust terminated due to the death of Ms. Gicovate. The Court took the accounting petition under submission and set a status date of January 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the filing of the petition for final accounting.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Aug-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Timothy Watson for and with Edward Watson (Trustee). Sheila Robello on behalf of Arthur Park (Guardian Ad Litem) for Alice Gicovate.

Ms. Robello explained that Mr. Park is on vacation and the he filed a declaration. Timothy Watson stated that the amount of fees requested are reasonable given the circumstances and the amount of work performed, and stated that it was a benefit to the Trust Estate and a savings to the Trust. Ms. Robello stated that the fees were much larger than the Court guideline fees, that these were general services of a Trustee, that the Trustee's rate was that of an Attorney and that there is an Attorney in this case. Edward Watson explained the process taken regarding sale of the two properties. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (1001 Pine Street, #1301, S. F., Ca 94109)
Off Calendar Dec-05-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Timothy Watson for and with Edward Watson (Trustee). Jan Small from Keynote Properties in Propria Persona. Yasuko Okubo (Buyer) in Propria Persona.

The Court informed Counsel that it does not allow vesting title to be determined in escrow. Ms. Okubo stated that she will take title in her name as a single woman, that she does not agree to Ms. Small receiving the entire (100% of the) 5% commissions, that she did not have an Agent when the agreement was signed, that she now has an agent and requested a credit of 2.5%. Ms. Small stated that she does not agree to Ms. Okubo receiving a credit of 2.5% of the commissions. Counsel read language from the sales contract. The Court stated that at the time the contract was signed there was no Agent, that the Report of Sale was served on Ms. Okubo and that the contract shows that Keynote is to receive 100% of the commissions. Ms. Okubo was given the option to back out of the sale but elected to proceed with the sale. The sale was confirmed at $521,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale November 6, 2013 except that title will be vested in the name of Yasuko Keiko Okubo as a single woman. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order and the order being revised). Bond is not required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Mr. Watson stated that the two units (1312 and 1301) are contiguous and were the Trustor's residence, that they want to sell them together and treat is as a residence, and take the $500,000.00 exclusion. The sale was confirmed at $410,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed October 11, 2013. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). No additional bond is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. (Not Reported)

Petition To Bring Revocable Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Sep-21-2010 Granted Per Written Order

Petition To Bring Revocable Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Sep-07-2010 Continued To Sep-21-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-14-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Watson in Propria Persona.

Mr. Watson requested the Court drop the petition and not bring the Trust under Court supervision stating that he filed a Conservatorship petition appointing Debra Dolch as the Conservator of the person of Bernard Gicovate and appointing himself and Ms. Dolch as the Co-Conservators of Mr. Bernard's estate. In addition, Mr. Watson stated that Ms. Gicovate signed the consent for the petition and agreed to allow her community property be covered under that conservatorship, that Ms. Gicovate was confused and thought that by having him appointed as trustee of the Trust that it would take away the assets from her Son and reported that Mr. Bernard was not doing well. Furthermore, Mr. Watson stated that there were three parcels of real property, two condominiums and one townhouse which were in horrible condition and not producing any income, and that the only liquid asset was a bank account which was being used to pay for her bills and their care which costed $15,000.00 per month. The matter was taken off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Apr-07-2010 Continued To Apr-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-24-2010 Continued To Apr-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Watson (Trustee) in Propria Persona. Arthur Park Attorney not currently representing any Party. Alice Gicovate in Propria Persona.

Mr. Watson stated that he consolidated a number of bank accounts, that Ms. Gicovate's needs are being met and that the Caregivers suggested Ms. Gicovate contact Mr. Park. In addition, Mr. Watson stated that issues still needed to be worked out, that it would be appropriate to have a conservatorship, that he contacted Debra Dolch, that they would request least restrictive means and expressed concern about Mr. Gicovate's care. Mr. Park stated that he met with Ms. Gicovate, that he was unable to determine what her conerns are, that she was upset with Mr. Watson and that he has not reviewed the trust documents. In addition, Mr. Park stated that he could not accept personal retention of Ms. Gicovate, that he could be a Guardian Ad Litem or that he could serve as Court appointed Counsel under a conservatorship. The Court stated that it could not order Mr. Watson to pursue a conservatorship and that it would be appropriate to bring the Trust under Court supervision. The matter was continued to April 7, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status of the Trust administration.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-10-2010 Continued To Feb-24-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Confirm Rejection Of Trust, To Remove Successor Trustee For Failure Or Declination To Act, And To Appoint Successor Trustee
Off Calendar Dec-28-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward Watson in Propria Persona. Alice Gicovate in Propria Persona.

This was a pre-granted matter. Mr. Watson stated that the Husband resided at the Coventry Park home, that Ms. Gicovate declined to act as her own Trustee, that he contacted her Son Henry to take over but that he refused, and that Ms. Gicovate asked to terminate the trust. In addition, he stated that Mr. and Mrs. Gicovate had significant care expenses, that money was needed as the the Power of Attorney account was out of money, that he was in contact with the Public Guardian and that they would file a petition for conservatorship if Adult Protective Services made a referal to them. Ms. Gicovate expressed that she wanted someone else to serve, that she was worried about the paper work and stated that she did not know what they were doing. The Court granted the petition without prejudice to allow Ms. Gicovate the opportunity to present her concerns to Mr. Watson and time to determine if she wants to hire Counsel or if she wants Mr. Watson to remain as Trustee (subject to review of the order). Status of administration of the trust was set for February 10, 2010.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.
